task_name: Add Local MVP Chain Checkpoint Through Live Builder Adapter Readiness
goal: Document the current local MVP chain through Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate and define the next safe boundary without enabling live Builder access, real task creation, execution, auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, DB, deploy, approve, or push.
claims: BP-076 documents the local MVP chain through Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate.
BP-076 identifies which chain stages are locally reviewable and which actions remain blocked.
BP-076 does not implement live Builder access, task creation, execution, approval recording, auth, secrets, persistence, UI, DB, deploy, approve, or push.
assumptions: BP-075 completed fixture coverage visibility for the Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate stage.
A checkpoint is needed before expanding toward any live adapter implementation or mutation boundary.
The next safe step should be a hardening decision, not live Builder implementation.
dod: The document states the local chain stages, current review suite entry point, blocked actions, open gaps, and next safe step.
The document explicitly blocks live Builder access, Builder Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, DB, UI, and target file writes.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Live Builder access, task creation, execution, approval recording, auth implementation, secrets, persistence, DB, deploy, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-075 made Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate fixture coverage visible and cleared the way for a local MVP chain checkpoint.
